handoff_profile.non_kernel_size_rule: exact file bytes, never allocation padding
handoff_profile.digest_rule: SHA-256 over the exact retained file bytes

producer_order: parse source file under its bounded format
copy exact bytes into final retained pages
reparse and cross-bind from the final retained pages
bind exact role, physical base, file length, and SHA-256 into PBP1
prove final-map coverage and identity translation before ExitBootServices
kernel_order: decode PBP1 and require successful ExitBootServices
require the exact ten-role profile and bounded flags
reject invalid, overflowing, or overlapping physical ranges
form slices only after the complete locator and size profile passes
match each exact slice to its PBP1 role, physical base, length, and digest
parse exact PSM1 and bind slot, kernel digest, image size, six roles, lengths, and digests
parse all six exact PBART1 envelopes and six inner contracts
parse exact PBTP1 and PBTS1 and reconstruct all PBTRUST1 observations
require the exact pbtrust_policy_unsigned denial and zero authority, actions, and state writes
non_claims: No private key was generated or used and no signature was created or verified by PKREVAL1.
The retained PBTS1 file remains an ESP development candidate, not selected PBSTATE1 persistent authority.
This standalone PKREVAL1 receipt does not execute PooleKernel; the separate PKXFER1 receipt owns the QEMU live-entry and guest-revalidation claim.
No capability, lifecycle, recovery, symbol, microcode, firmware, policy, or state-write authority is granted.
No target firmware, physical hardware, second builder, signed release, ISO, installer, or production promotion is claimed.
